heroHeadline: Starship V3 Flight 12 Shifts to May; Orion Heat Shield Inspection Enters Week Three
heroSubtitle: Apr 26: Seventeen days after Artemis II's April 10 splashdown, the Artemis program advances on three parallel fronts. At KSC's Multi-Payload Processing Facility, the formal 30-day Orion heat shield inspection progresses through week three — Marshall Space Flight Center X-ray scans and sample extractions are underway, with results expected ~May 10 defining the Artemis IV 2028 crewed lunar landing timeline. At Starbase, SpaceX confirmed Starship V3 (Flight 12) cleared all static fire milestones by April 15 and shifted its window from late April to May 2026, with FAA license approval pending — a critical next step in Artemis III HLS qualification. In Washington, congressional appropriators continue reviewing the proposed $18.8B FY2027 budget (23% cut, 47% science reduction) with bipartisan opposition from Chair Babin (R-TX) and Ranking Member Lofgren (D-CA) expected to soften the deepest science cuts. The Artemis II crew continues physiological reconditioning at JSC.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
